body:
Welcome to the Product Sense Gym! We're excited to have you join our community for sharp, bite-sized product judgment practice. Expect daily prompts covering the tricky tradeoffs, prioritization puzzles, onboarding tweaks, pricing decisions, and launch choices that define great products.

Every answer you give gets a score based on depth and creativity. We'll highlight top responses and discuss different approaches in the threads. This is your space to hone your product intuition, learn from others, and get better at making tough calls.

Jump in, share your thoughts on today's prompt, and let's build some product sense together!

Last week was a fantastic set of reps, pushing our product sense to new heights. We tackled a range of tricky scenarios, from late-stage feature prioritization to early-stage pricing models.

The trickiest question involved deciding whether to double down on an existing core feature or invest in a brand-new, potentially disruptive one. What made it so challenging was the inherent uncertainty. The existing feature had proven value but was hitting a growth ceiling, while the new feature offered higher potential upside but carried significant technical and market risks. Participants had to weigh direct evidence against speculative futures, a classic product dilemma that truly tested our judgment.

Great work everyone pushing through these tough choices. Keep those reps up!

This week's resource is the book "Obviously Awesome" by April Dunford. It's a fantastic guide to product positioning, helping you articulate your product's value in a way that resonates with your target market. Dunford provides a clear, actionable framework for understanding your customers and competitors, and then crafting a compelling narrative. If you're wrestling with how to describe your product, or struggling to cut through the noise, this book is a valuable investment of your time. It helps you think critically about tradeoffs in your messaging, a core skill for Product Sense Gym members.

Dunford’s approach focuses on practical exercises and real-world examples, making it easy to apply the concepts to your own product challenges. It’s less about theory and more about building the actual position for your product. This directness aligns perfectly with the 'short reps' ethos of the Gym.

Big congrats to @top_scorer for absolutely crushing it in the Product Sense Gym this past week! Your sharp insights and strategic thinking really stood out in the challenges.

Keep up the fantastic work. Those reps are clearly paying off, and you're setting a high bar for all of us.

We're excited to see how you continue to hone your product judgment next week!

Product Sense Gym has officially hit 200 members! It's been inspiring to see this community grow and to witness the sharp product thinking and constructive feedback shared here.

Thanks to everyone who has joined the gym and put in the reps. We're excited to continue building this space together, tackling challenging product trade-offs and refining our instincts with each prompt.

We're just getting started. Let's keep the momentum going!
